preamble: The transformation of [ɛ] to [oi] is a complex shift involving backing, rounding, and diphthongization. It typically occurs in environments where a front vowel is influenced by neighboring labial or velar consonants, or as part of a larger vowel chain shift.

examples.note: Backing and rounding of a front vowel into a diphthong.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
